worth_it_explanation: Amazon owned-and-operated supply, including Amazon.com, IMDb, Twitch, Fire TV, and Prime Video, is where Amazon's first-party shopping signals and its ad inventory sit closest together. Whether a line item buys that supply intentionally or avoids it without reason has a direct effect on reach, cost, and measurement accuracy. The checks here are fast: the Amazon DSP console surfaces supply source breakdowns in the reporting UI, and streaming TV (STV) line item types are visible in the line item settings. The fixes are configuration changes, not rebuilds.
intro: Amazon owned-and-operated (O&O) supply covers inventory on Amazon.com, IMDb, Twitch, Fire TV, and Prime Video. These placements sit alongside or inside Amazon's own shopping and entertainment experiences, which means audience signals, measurement, and creative requirements all behave differently here than they do on third-party exchange inventory. These checks confirm that O&O supply is used with a clear rationale, that STV and Prime Video placements are configured with the right line item type and creative format, and that the mix of O&O and third-party supply reflects the campaign's stated objective rather than a default setting no one has revisited.

audit_checks.title: Amazon owned-and-operated supply is on or off by default rather than set deliberately
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Amazon O&O inventory, particularly Amazon.com and Prime Video, carries premium cost-per-mille rates justified by high-intent audiences. Leaving it on when the campaign goal is broad reach at efficient cost inflates spend without a corresponding gain. Turning it off entirely on an endemic campaign sacrifices the highest-intent placements available to that advertiser.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP gives advertisers access to Amazon owned-and-operated (O&O) supply alongside third-party exchange inventory. Amazon O&O includes Amazon.com, IMDb, Twitch, Fire TV, and Prime Video. Each surface carries distinct audience composition, creative requirements, and pricing, and each one serves a different role in the funnel.

Amazon.com display placements reach active shoppers while they search and browse, which makes them well-suited to endemic advertisers, meaning those who sell products on Amazon, trying to influence purchase decisions near the point of sale. IMDb and Twitch attract entertainment and gaming audiences, respectively, and tend to serve brand-awareness goals. Fire TV and Prime Video placements appear in a TV viewing context and support both awareness and retargeting across the household.

Whether O&O supply is included in a line item depends on the supply source settings at the line item level. Amazon DSP lets advertisers configure supply by type: Amazon O&O, Amazon Publisher Services-distributed inventory, and open-auction third-party inventory can be toggled separately. A line item that includes all three by default without deliberate review may over-weight expensive O&O inventory on a budget-sensitive campaign, or under-weight it on a campaign where Amazon shopping intent is the primary signal being purchased.

A few signs point to O&O supply being configured by default rather than deliberately:
  • All line items in an order include O&O supply regardless of their objective or audience
  • No line item in an endemic campaign explicitly prioritizes Amazon.com display placements
  • Reporting shows negligible O&O delivery despite no intentional exclusion, suggesting the floor pricing on O&O is clearing out the budget before O&O can compete
You may want to review the supply source settings on each line item alongside its goal and audience before concluding the configuration is correct.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser selling Amazon Standard Identification Numbers (ASINs) on Amazon, O&O supply, particularly Amazon.com, is where shopping-intent signals are most concentrated. You may want to confirm that at least one line item in the order is explicitly configured to include Amazon.com O&O supply and is paced to deliver there rather than defaulting away to cheaper third-party exchange inventory.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser driving traffic off Amazon, Amazon O&O supply still provides reach across high-engagement environments like IMDb and Prime Video, but the premium cost-per-mille rates are harder to justify without the purchase-signal upside endemic advertisers get. You may want to review whether O&O is included and whether the campaign's target cost-per-acquisition supports its pricing relative to third-party alternatives.

learn_more.source_title: Maximize campaign performance with the Amazon owned-and-operated network
Ad specs: Ad sizes and policies

investigate.intro: Review each line item's supply source settings and cross-reference them with the line item's objective and audience to confirm that O&O inclusion or exclusion was a deliberate decision.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the order in the Amazon DSP console and navigate to the line items list.
For each line item, open its settings and locate the supply source section. Note which supply types are included: Amazon O&O, Amazon Publisher Services, and third-party open exchange.

Compare the supply source settings against the line item's objective and audience. A line item targeting in-market shoppers for an endemic campaign that excludes O&O is worth flagging. A brand-awareness line item with a tight cost-per-mille target that includes O&O alongside all third-party sources is also worth reviewing.

Pull a supply source performance report in the reporting UI. Set the dimension to 'Supply source' or 'Inventory type' and review delivery share, cost-per-mille, and conversion rate by source. Note any source with high spend and low conversion that may not belong in the mix.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per line item, the supply source configuration and the line item objective. A line item whose supply sources are set deliberately to match its goal passes. A line item where O&O is included or excluded because it was the default and no one reviewed it is a fail, recorded with the line item name and the supply type at issue.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the supply source setting and the objective for each line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that Amazon owned-and-operated supply on {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaigns is configured by default rather than deliberately. The supply source settings on the reviewed line items do not reflect a considered decision about where the campaign's budget should compete. For an endemic advertiser, this may mean Amazon.com placements are under-represented where they would carry the most purchase-intent signal; for a non-endemic advertiser, it may mean premium O&O inventory is absorbing budget that a lower-cost third-party mix could serve as effectively. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the supply source settings on each line item alongside its objective and pulling a supply source performance breakdown to confirm that O&O inclusion or exclusion is supported by the data.

audit_checks.title: Streaming TV and Prime Video placements are not using the correct line item type
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Streaming TV (STV) and Prime Video require a video line item type configured for those environments. A display line item targeting the same inventory will either fail to deliver, serve the wrong creative format, or reach Fire TV and Prime Video screens with a creative that does not meet spec. Mismatched creative and line item type also blocks Amazon Attribution measurement for those placements.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP structures line items by inventory type. STV and Prime Video placements require a video line item type, not a standard display line item. The video line item type controls which creative formats are accepted, which supply sources are eligible, and how the placement renders on connected-TV devices including Fire TV and Prime Video screens.

A display line item cannot serve on STV or Prime Video. If a campaign is intended to run on those surfaces but the line item type is display, the order may show delivery against Fire TV or IMDb display placements without ever reaching the living-room video context the campaign was planned for. The confusion arises because Fire TV offers both display placements (feature rotator, sponsored tiles) and video placements (STV pre-roll and mid-roll). The two are different inventory types and require different line item configurations.

Prime Video advertising, available as a placement through Amazon DSP, requires a video creative of at least 15 seconds formatted to the Prime Video specification. Submitting a display creative or a video creative outside the accepted dimensions means the creative does not run on Prime Video even when the line item targets it.

A few signs point to a line item type mismatch:
  • A campaign planned to run on Prime Video or STV is delivering exclusively on display placements
  • Creative uploaded to a line item that targets Fire TV or Prime Video is a static image or an HTML5 banner
  • The line item settings show a display type with Prime Video or STV supply sources checked, which is a configuration the console allows but which will not deliver on those surfaces
You may want to confirm the line item type in the console settings before reviewing creative compliance. A type mismatch is the first thing that blocks STV delivery.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser running STV or Prime Video to drive upper-funnel awareness for an Amazon product, a line item type mismatch means the living-room video touchpoint is absent from the campaign and the audience it reaches is narrower than planned. You may want to confirm that video line items are present and delivering alongside any display line items targeting Fire TV.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser using Prime Video or STV to build brand awareness, a line item type mismatch is a complete gap in the planned media. You may want to confirm that the video line item exists, is active, and that the creative meets Prime Video's format and duration requirements.

learn_more.source_title: Fire TV ad specifications and guidelines
Prime Video ad specifications and guidelines

investigate.intro: Confirm that every line item targeting STV, Fire TV video, or Prime Video uses a video line item type and that the associated creative meets the format and duration requirements for those surfaces.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the order in the Amazon DSP console. For each line item, open its settings and note the line item type, for example 'Display' or 'Video'.

If the line item name or goal references STV, Fire TV, Prime Video, or streaming, confirm the type is 'Video' not 'Display'. A display type on a line item intended for video inventory will not deliver on STV or Prime Video.

Navigate to the creative assignments for each video line item. Confirm the attached creative is a video asset (not a static image or HTML5 banner), note its duration, and compare it against the accepted duration for the targeted surface. Prime Video requires video creative of at least 15 seconds; STV placements on Fire TV pre-roll typically require 15 or 30 seconds.

Pull a placement or supply source report for the order. If a line item targeting STV shows zero video impressions but positive display impressions on Fire TV, the type is set to display and the video surfaces are not delivering.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per line item targeting STV, Fire TV video, or Prime Video, the line item type and the creative format attached. A video line item with a video creative meeting the duration and dimension requirements for the targeted surface passes. A display line item targeting those surfaces, or a video line item with a non-conforming creative, is a fail recorded with the line item name and the specific mismatch.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the line item type and creative format for each STV, Fire TV, or Prime Video line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more line items on {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaigns targeting streaming TV (STV) or Prime Video are not using the correct line item type or creative format. A display line item cannot serve on STV or Prime Video, and a video creative that does not meet the surface's duration or dimension requirements will not deliver there either. The result is that the living-room video placements planned for the campaign are absent from actual delivery. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the line item type for each STV and Prime Video line item in the Amazon DSP console, confirming that a video type is set, and verifying that the attached creative meets the accepted format and duration for each targeted surface.

audit_checks.title: The mix of Amazon O&O and third-party supply does not match the campaign objective
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Supply mix affects cost efficiency and measurement fidelity together. A campaign built primarily on third-party exchange inventory cannot take advantage of Amazon's closed-loop measurement, which requires delivery on Amazon O&O surfaces to link ad exposure to purchase. A campaign relying entirely on O&O supply may overpay for reach goals that open exchange could serve at lower cost-per-mille.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP's supply mix, the share of impressions delivered on Amazon O&O versus third-party exchange inventory, has measurement consequences that go beyond reach and cost. Amazon's closed-loop reporting, which links ad exposure to downstream Amazon purchases and detail page views, works only when the ad was served on Amazon O&O or on inventory within Amazon Publisher Services. Impressions delivered on open third-party exchange inventory do not generate purchase signals that feed back into Amazon Attribution or Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC).

The supply mix is both an efficiency lever and a measurement lever. A campaign whose performance is evaluated against Amazon conversion metrics, for example new-to-brand purchases or Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) detail page views, needs enough O&O delivery to generate the measurement signal. A campaign primarily on third-party exchange will show impression and click data but little or no Amazon conversion data, not because the campaign did not convert but because the measurement path depends on O&O delivery.

Conversely, a campaign built entirely on O&O supply for a broad-reach awareness goal, particularly one measured by cost-per-thousand impressions, may pay a premium over what third-party exchange would cost for comparable demographics. O&O inventory on Amazon.com and Prime Video commands higher floor prices because of the audience intent signals attached to it, and those premiums make sense for conversion goals but are harder to justify for pure reach.

A few signs point to a supply mix that does not match the objective:
  • A campaign measured on Amazon purchase conversions delivers more than 80% of its impressions on third-party exchange, leaving AMC with too small a sample to report on
  • A broad-reach awareness campaign with a cost-per-mille target runs primarily on Amazon.com O&O, paying intent-audience rates for a goal that does not require intent-level signals
  • No review of the supply source breakdown has taken place since the campaign launched
You may want to pull a supply source performance breakdown and compare the delivery mix against the primary key performance indicator before deciding the mix is working.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser measuring AMC-derived new-to-brand purchases or ASIN detail page views, a supply mix that skews heavily toward third-party exchange will generate impressions and clicks but limited AMC conversion data. You may want to confirm that a meaningful share of delivery lands on Amazon O&O to support the measurement path the campaign was designed around.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser with no Amazon conversion goal, the measurement consequence of third-party exchange delivery is less acute since there is no Amazon purchase signal to capture. You may want to review the mix primarily as a cost-efficiency question: whether O&O is earning its premium cost-per-mille relative to the reach and frequency that third-party exchange delivers at lower cost.

learn_more.source_title: Maximize campaign performance with the Amazon owned-and-operated network

investigate.intro: Pull a supply source performance report and compare the O&O versus third-party split against the campaign's primary measurement goal to confirm the mix is intentional and defensible.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the reporting section in the Amazon DSP console. Create a report with the supply source or inventory type dimension for the order or campaign you are reviewing. Set the date range to the full active period.
Note the percentage of impressions and spend delivered on Amazon O&O versus Amazon Publisher Services versus third-party open exchange.

Compare the delivery split against the campaign's primary key performance indicator. If the goal is AMC-measured conversion and fewer than 30 to 40 percent of impressions land on O&O, flag the mix for review. If the goal is reach at a cost-per-mille target and more than 60 to 70 percent of spend is on O&O, flag the cost efficiency.

If the account has Amazon Marketing Cloud access, check whether the AMC dataset for the order has sufficient O&O impressions to run path-to-conversion queries. Too few O&O impressions will produce incomplete or suppressed AMC reports.

amazon_marketing_cloud.label: Amazon Marketing Cloud
steps: If AMC access is available, run a basic impression overlap query for the order to see how many unique users were reached on O&O versus third-party supply.

Note whether the O&O audience has a higher conversion rate on AMC-attributed conversions than the third-party portion. A large gap suggests the O&O inventory is earning its premium; a negligible gap suggests the third-party mix could carry more of the load.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the O&O delivery share and the campaign's primary measurement goal. An endemic campaign measured on Amazon conversions with at least 30 to 40 percent O&O delivery passes. A non-endemic campaign measured on reach or cost-per-mille with a defensible O&O inclusion rationale passes. Any campaign where the supply mix has never been reviewed or where the O&O share is clearly misaligned with the goal is a fail recorded with the order name and the specific mismatch.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the O&O delivery share and the primary measurement goal for the order above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the mix of Amazon owned-and-operated supply and third-party exchange inventory on {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaigns does not align with the stated campaign objective. A campaign measured on Amazon Marketing Cloud conversion data requires enough O&O delivery to generate the measurement signal; a campaign measured on reach efficiency may be over-weighting premium O&O inventory relative to what third-party exchange can provide at lower cost. {{voice_recommend}} pulling a supply source breakdown report and reviewing the O&O versus third-party split against the primary key performance indicator, then adjusting supply source settings on the affected line items to better match the delivery mix to the goal.
